div.p: The loss surface is a high-dimensional terrain. Gradient descent is a ball rolling downhill.

g.text: <text x="80" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="#27ae60" font-weight="bold">Global Min</text>
<text x="200" y="105" text-anchor="middle" font-size="9" fill="#e67e22">Local Min</text>

ul.li: Minus: We go opposite to gradient

div.p: ∇L = [∂L/∂w₁, ∂L/∂w₂, ..., ∂L/∂b] Points in direction of steepest ascent. To decrease loss, move opposite:
